flashcards.definition: A disturbance that transfers energy through water; caused primarily by wind; water particles move in orbits.
The distance over open water that wind blows in a constant direction; longer fetch = larger waves.
Highest tidal range when Sun, Moon, and Earth align (new and full moon); gravitational forces combine.
Lowest tidal range when Sun and Moon are at right angles (quarter moons); gravitational forces partially cancel.
Current flowing parallel to the shore created by waves approaching at an angle; transports sediment along the coast.
